items.title: Implement OpenAI Embeddings Probe in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an end-to-end smoke probe for OpenAI embeddings using the existing continuous probe framework. A new production test deployment has been added to run the embeddings probe every minute, logging embedding vector lengths and token usage for monitoring purposes. Key files modified include the deployment configuration and test scripts to support this functionality.

items.title: Clean Up Unused Files in Index 🧹
items.summary: This update removes several stale root-level files from the Index repository, including `.DS_Store`, `yarn.lock`, and various logo images. These files were either unnecessary or already managed in submodules, streamlining the repository and improving clarity. The essential files as outlined in the README remain intact.

items.title: Enhance .gitignore and Document X402 Test Command in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces important changes to the `.gitignore` file by adding entries for `.tmp/`, `SDK/`, `SolRadar/`, and `X402Client/`, ensuring that unnecessary files are not tracked. Additionally, a new documentation file for the X402 payment-flow test procedure has been added, providing clear guidelines for testing related changes. These updates streamline project management and enhance clarity for developers.

items.title: Streamline API Usage Section by Removing Top Services in Roadmap 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the 'Top Services by Usage' list from the API Usage section, ceasing its publication in the generated API usage snapshot. The Roadmap copy and historical update text have been updated to reflect this change, ensuring no references to the removed feature remain. This streamlining enhances clarity and focus on key metrics.

items.title: Upgrade uuid to version 14.0.0 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the uuid package from version 13.0.0 to 14.0.0, introducing breaking changes that require Node.js version 20 or higher. The update drops support for Node.js 18 and enhances security by ensuring that certain functions validate buffer boundaries. Overall, this upgrade improves compatibility and security for the project.

items.title: Automated research cycle completed in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest automated research cycle for April 30, 2026, has been successfully completed, resulting in the addition of a new report file. A total of 22 candidates were reviewed, with 9 candidates on hold and 13 new listings eliminated due to various risk factors. Notable eliminations included tokens like SNOWBALL and TRUTH, which were flagged for significant risks.

items.title: New Research Cycle Update in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle has been completed, reviewing 29 candidates with key decisions made on several tokens. Notably, Oilland has been recommended for a buy, while 12 candidates were skipped due to low liquidity. The report includes detailed scoring and methodology for assessing each candidate's potential.

items.title: OpenAI service updates fully integrated in SDK 🚀
items.summary: The recent updates to the OpenAI specification have been reviewed, confirming that both the TypeScript and Python SDKs are already compatible with the latest changes. The only modification was to the `size` field, which now accepts any free-form dimensions, enhancing flexibility for image generation. All existing endpoints remain intact with no additional changes required.

items.title: Enhance Image Size Flexibility in M C Ps 🎨
items.summary: This update modifies the image size parameter for OpenAI image generation and editing, transitioning from a fixed enum to a flexible free-form string format. Key changes include updates to type definitions and documentation to support custom dimensions, allowing for more creative freedom in image generation. The new constraints ensure dimensions remain within specified limits, enhancing usability for developers.

items.title: Enhance chat command with new OpenAPI parameters in Clis 🚀
items.summary: The chat command in the CLI has been updated to include four new options: `--store`, `--logprobs`, `--top-logprobs`, and `--parallel-tool-calls`. These enhancements align the CLI with the latest OpenAPI spec for the `POST /openai/chat/completions` endpoint. Note that some complex parameters were omitted due to their requirement for structured JSON input.

items.title: Updated OpenAI Docs for Image Generation and Edits in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the latest changes from upstream OpenAI documentation regarding the `gpt-image-2` size acceptance and billing tiers. Key clarifications include the acceptance of any `WIDTHxHEIGHT` format for sizes, along with explicit billing details for standard and custom sizes. Additionally, a note on output aliasing and an updated example asset URL have been included to enhance user understanding.

items.title: Enhance Nano Banana Validation Script in Index 🚀
items.summary: We've added a new validation script, validate_nano.py, to assist with production checks for Nano Banana. This script facilitates callback-mode image generation and editing, while generating comprehensive JSON and Markdown reports detailing request payloads, responses, and image data. This enhancement aims to streamline validation processes and improve reporting accuracy.

items.title: Enhanced GPT-Image-2 Billing and Size Flexibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the strict 15-preset whitelist for `gpt-image-2` sizes, introducing a two-tier billing model. Users can now utilize a broader range of dimensions, with specific billing rates for standard and custom sizes. Key changes include updates to pricing rules, API documentation, and test coverage enhancements.

items.title: Expanded GPT Image Size Options in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the strict 15-preset whitelist for `gpt-image-2` sizes, allowing any valid `WIDTHxHEIGHT` format. Users will now be billed at the 1K rate for specific sizes while other custom dimensions will fall under a new 'other' tier at 1.5x. The changes enhance flexibility in image requests and align billing with actual usage.

items.title: Revamp Custom Connector Management UI in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the connections table by adding provider details, scope tags, and timestamps for expiration and refresh. Custom MCP connectors are now treated as server/resource connections, and a new dialog for adding custom connectors has been introduced. Additionally, backend errors are now surfaced on the OAuth callback page, improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Support for Custom Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for manual OAuth client credentials in custom MCP connectors, allowing for a display name and the selection of token endpoint authentication methods based on server metadata. Dynamic client registration remains the default when no manual client ID is provided. Key files modified include serializers, services, and views to accommodate these changes.

items.title: Enhance gpt-image-2 Validator for 1K Outputs in Index 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the `gpt-image-2` validator by introducing a new flag that allows for a +/-2% tolerance in aspect ratio checks for 1K outputs, addressing previous pixel-imprecision issues. Additionally, the fetch mechanism now attempts a Range request before falling back to a standard GET, enhancing compatibility with CDN edges. The overall validation suite has passed successfully, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Automated i18n Updates from Chinese Sources in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces automatic translation updates for internationalization (i18n) based on changes from the Chinese (zh-CN) source. Key modifications include updates to multiple language files, ensuring that translations are current and accurate across various languages including Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese. This enhancement improves user experience by providing timely and relevant translations.

items.title: Enhance Gemini Fallback for Backup Providers in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enables the native fallback for Gemini capabilities across multiple backup providers, including xiaojingai, openaihk, and ephone. By marking the relevant models as native, the system can now utilize the multi-provider failover during outages, significantly improving reliability. This change addresses previous issues where only one provider was eligible for fallback, ensuring smoother operations during disruptions.

items.title: Update invitees page title for clarity in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update corrects the title on the console distribution invitees page, changing it from 'Inviter' to 'Invitees' in English and from '邀请人' to '受邀用户' in Simplified Chinese. Additionally, translator comments have been updated to clarify that the page lists users invited by the current user. These changes enhance user understanding and improve localization accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Image Validation with New Skill in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `/validate-image-2` skill, which validates the `gpt-image-2` image generation and editing endpoints across 14 production scenarios. Key features include comprehensive testing for various image sizes and formats, with detailed reporting on the outcomes. The implementation ensures robust performance and reliability for image processing tasks.

items.title: Confirming OpenAI SDK Sync with Latest Docs Spec in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This PR verifies that the OpenAI SDK remains in sync with the latest updates to the documentation, requiring no code changes. Key updates include new enum values for image sizes and the restoration of the `dall-e-2` model in the edits model enum. Additionally, new parameters for chat completions have been documented, but the SDK's existing structure accommodates these changes seamlessly.

items.title: OpenAI CLI Sync Verified with Latest Docs Spec in Clis 🚀
items.summary: The OpenAI CLI has been confirmed to be in sync with the latest documentation specifications, with no changes required. All model enums and endpoint paths match the current spec, and 68 existing tests have successfully passed. This ensures that users can rely on the CLI's accuracy and functionality without any additional updates needed.

items.title: Updated OpenAI Docs: New Size/Billing Info in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update removes references to `gpt-image-1.5` and introduces a detailed size and billing tier table for `gpt-image-2` in the OpenAI documentation. Key changes include updates to the integration guides and the README file, ensuring users have the latest information on supported image sizes and billing structures. The new tables clarify the available resolutions and their respective billing tiers.

items.title: OpenAI service spec verified with no changes needed in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: The audit of the `openai` service spec confirms that the MCP server is fully in sync with the latest upstream Docs. Key components such as `ChatModel`, `ResponsesModel`, `ImageModel`, and `EmbeddingModel` have all been verified against the current specifications, showing complete alignment. No code changes were necessary as all enums and endpoint paths match the spec.

items.title: Enhance gpt-image-2 Size Handling in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update relaxes the size whitelist for the gpt-image-2 model, allowing 'auto' or omitted size parameters to be accepted. Key changes include the addition of cost tests for these scenarios and updates to the zh-CN documentation to reflect these adjustments. Overall, 698 tests pass without altering existing billing rules.

items.title: Enhance size handling for gpt-image-2 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This hotfix updates the handling of the `size` field in the gpt-image-2 model to accept both 'auto' and missing values, allowing upstream to determine the default size. The change ensures that existing presets continue to function while providing better flexibility for users. A successful test confirms that the new behavior works as intended without breaking existing functionality.

items.title: Streamline Nano Banana Edits in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the handling of Nano Banana edits with the verified behavior from Mountsea. It omits the auto aspect ratio when not specified by callers, ensuring the field remains unset for upstream while maintaining detection for logging. Additionally, the now-unnecessary snap-to-nearest aspect ratio helper has been removed to clean up the codebase.

items.title: Enhance Chat Completion with New Parameters in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new parameters, `reasoning_effort` and `service_tier`, to the chat completion tool, aligning with the latest OpenAPI spec. The `reasoning_effort` parameter allows users to control the intensity of reasoning for o-series models, while `service_tier` enables selection of the API processing tier. These enhancements improve flexibility and control for developers using the chat completion feature.

items.title: Enhance Image Editing with New Parameters in S D K 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces two new optional parameters, `mask` and `partial_images`, to the `images.edit()` endpoint for both TypeScript and Python SDKs. The `mask` parameter allows users to specify a PNG mask for editable regions, while `partial_images` enables streaming of partial image counts. These enhancements improve the flexibility and functionality of image editing capabilities.

items.title: Enhance CLI with New Reasoning and Service Tiers in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new options for the CLI's `chat` command, adding a `minimal` reasoning effort tier and `scale` and `priority` service tiers. The help text has been updated to reflect these changes, ensuring users are aware of all valid options. These enhancements keep the CLI in sync with the latest OpenAPI specifications.

items.title: Enhance CLI with Latest OpenAPI Features in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update synchronizes the CLI with the latest OpenAPI specifications for OpenAI, introducing new enum values for chat and image endpoints. Key additions include new reasoning effort options and service tiers for chat, as well as high-resolution size options for image generation and editing commands. These enhancements improve user experience and expand functionality.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Size Options in M C Ps 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces new 2K and 4K image size presets to align with the latest OpenAPI specifications for the OpenAI service. Key changes include expanded `ImageSize` and `ImageEditSize` types in `core/types.py`, updated parameter descriptions in `image_tools.py`, and an extended size table in `info_tools.py`. These enhancements improve the flexibility and options available for image generation and editing.

items.title: Update GPT-Image-2 Pricing and Size Support in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the GPT-Image-2 service by introducing tiered pricing for 2K and 4K sizes, correcting previous discrepancies on the public pricing page and developer documentation. Key changes include the addition of a new pricing rule for 2K/4K sizes and comprehensive documentation updates in Chinese, ensuring clarity on supported sizes and billing tiers. These improvements will streamline user experience and provide accurate information for developers.

items.title: Refine Mountsea Aspect Ratio Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the aspect ratio handling for Mountsea edits by omitting auto-detected ratios when not explicitly provided. The change ensures that the first input image ratio is preserved, enhancing the accuracy of image edits. Additionally, the validation for `aspect_ratio` remains intact, ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Enhance User Admin with Search and Filters in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the Django admin user list by adding a search box and filters. Admins can now easily look up users by ID, username, email, and more. Additionally, the user display has been updated to show email and phone numbers, enhancing usability.

items.title: Refine Mountsea Aspect Ratio Handling in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the previous broad fix for Nano Banana with a more focused solution for Mountsea's aspect ratio handling. The code now snaps auto-detected image ratios to the nearest supported values, ensuring compatibility with Mountsea's model-specific allowlist. This change maintains existing resolution forwarding for `nano-banana-2` and `nano-banana-pro`, while addressing issues with invalid aspect ratios in edit requests.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Callback with Connection ID in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the inclusion of `connection_id` in the custom OAuth redirect URI for better connection handling. By maintaining the same redirect URI in connection metadata, the token exchange process can now accurately identify pending connections, preventing generic error states. Key modifications were made in the connection service to support this functionality.

items.title: No SDK Changes Needed After OpenAI Docs Update in S D K 🚀
items.summary: The recent update to the OpenAI documentation introduced structural changes that do not require any modifications to the SDK. Key updates include the addition of `dall-e-2` to the model enum and new sizes for image edits and generations, both of which are already accommodated in the current SDK implementation. All OpenAI endpoints remain correctly mapped across TypeScript, Python, and Go.

items.title: Update OpenAI Docs for New Models in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update syncs the OpenAI documentation with recent changes from AceDataCloud, incorporating new models such as `gpt-5.5` and `gpt-image-1.5`. The `README.md` has been revised to reflect the removal of `gpt-5-pro` and to enhance the GPT-5.x description. Additionally, integration guides have been updated to include the latest models, ensuring comprehensive and accurate documentation.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI CLI with New Parameters in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces several new parameters to the OpenAI CLI, aligning it with the latest OpenAPI specifications. Key additions include options for the `chat`, `edit`, and `response` commands, enhancing functionality for users. Notably, new sampling controls and image editing features have been added, improving the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Size Options in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces 10 new high-resolution image size options to the OpenAI service, enhancing the flexibility for image generation and editing. Key changes include the addition of sizes such as 2048x2048 and 3840x2160 in the `openai/core/types.py` file, along with updated descriptions in the `openai/tools/image_tools.py`. These enhancements will allow users to create and edit images with greater detail and variety.

items.title: Exciting New Size Presets and Pricing for GPT-Image-2 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new size presets (1K, 2K, and 4K) for the GPT-Image-2 model, along with tiered pricing structures. The OpenAPI specifications have been updated to reflect these changes, allowing for 15 whitelisted size values. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure the accuracy of the new cost rules, all passing successfully.

items.title: Enhance gpt-image-2 with 1K/2K/4K Size Presets in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for 1K, 2K, and 4K size presets for the gpt-image-2 model in the TTAPI worker. The public API remains OpenAI-shaped, while the internal variant, gpt-image-2-plus, is utilized for higher resolutions. Key routing logic has been implemented to handle size requests effectively, ensuring compliance with upstream constraints.

items.title: New Research Cycle Results in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: The latest research cycle has been completed, adding a new report file with insights from 20 candidates. Key findings include a hold recommendation for EMC2 due to strong buy pressure, while several candidates were skipped due to low liquidity and coordinated bot activity. The report highlights the importance of social confirmation for potential upgrades.

items.title: Enhance request ID generation for better compatibility in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue with request ID generation in WebView environments lacking `crypto.randomUUID`. The method has been switched to use the `uuid` package, ensuring consistent functionality across platforms. This change improves the reliability of API requests in affected environments.

items.title: Enhance request ID generation for better compatibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue with generating request IDs in certain WebView environments, such as WeChat, where `crypto.randomUUID` is unavailable. The solution involves switching to the `uuid` package for generating `x-request-id`, ensuring smoother API requests across different platforms. Validation steps have been completed to confirm the changes.

items.title: Fix request ID generation for WeChat compatibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue with the WeChat WebView environment where `crypto.randomUUID` is unavailable, causing axios request interceptors to fail. The solution involved switching the generation of `x-request-id` from `crypto.randomUUID()` to the `uuid` package, ensuring smoother login requests. The changes have been validated with linting and a successful build.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Connector Support in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for custom OAuth/MCP connectors on the Connections page of the AuthFrontend. Key additions include new operator methods for authorization and exchange, as well as a callback endpoint for handling OAuth responses. Locale files for Chinese and English have been updated to reflect these changes, enhancing user experience.
